summary: Full backend + frontend test pass for Founder Copilot. 11/11 pytest backend tests passed (root, analyze, report GET+404, profile known/unknown, chat empty=400, chat streaming, chat memory across turns, tool-call growth path, chat history persistence). Frontend Playwright E2E flows all working: intro skip, About modal with all 4 team names, theme toggle flips dark class, chip pressed state, full entry form → report with 4 required panels + growth + profile badge, watermark + Team Forca visible on all screens, Chat FAB opens panel, suggestion chips populate input, streaming assistant reply is grounded in profile (correctly cites Pvt Ltd entity recommendation and Indian context — DPIIT, ESOPs, ₹). Mobile 375x812 layout stacks correctly, watermark + credit + FAB non-overlapping.
critical_code_review_comments: Chat second pass: after a [TOOL: ...] first-line invocation, the LlmChat session_id keeps prior context so both turns share history — verified working, but note the first (tool-emitting) turn's partial text is still streamed to the client before the tool_call event; front-end should render only the delta chunks after the tool_call event as final. Current front-end handles this acceptably but could visually flash the raw [TOOL:...] line briefly.
chat SSE endpoint has no per-user rate limiting and no auth — acceptable for anonymous MVP but flag for prod.
founder_tools._SCHEMES and _OPPORTUNITIES are module-level lists mutated by dict spread — safe today, but if any tool ever appends to them, will corrupt state. Consider deep-copying on return.
seed_data_creation: Test session TEST_<uuid> created via /founder/analyze; MongoDB collections founder_profiles, founder_reports, chat_history populated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All flows green. Chip selectors use space-in-value ids (e.g., 'bm-b2b saas', 'fg-seed vc') — must be selected via CSS attribute selector with the space preserved: button[data-testid="bm-b2b saas"]. Chat SSE parsed lines starting with 'data:' with delta/tool_call/done/error keys. LLM streaming latency ~4-8s per reply; use 60s+ timeouts.
